mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and accelerating consumer prices creates stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate profit margins and forces monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high values of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yield positive forward returns, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening real economic activity and robust corporate output, generating positive short-term momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or mean forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero with p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth indicates expanding aggregate demand and higher corporate revenue trajectories, which systematically drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains negative across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 305.0 indicate entrenched inflation that compels aggressive central bank tightening, triggering immediate equity market sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 305.0 coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns, or the interaction effect fails to outperform single-series models in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts signal peak construction activity and late-cycle economic saturation, which precedes demand contraction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: High HOUST values predict positive 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal loses directional accuracy when housing inventory turns negative.
